steps.title: Spot the defining evidence
steps.content: The strongest clue is the supporting evidence introduced by the colon: “nearly a dozen of the characters that populate his novels are poets or writers.” This demonstrates that poets are common in Delany's work.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: ”Atypical” means unusual or not typical, so “is not atypical” means typical and normal. For an occupation frequently appearing in an author's novels, “typical” is the right characterization. The other options fail because they address unrelated concepts: infallible (about mistakes), lucrative (about money), tedious (about boredom). That matches choice B — B is correct.
